name: Freezing Melons
recipeInstructions:
1. Wash and cut the melon into small pieces.
2. Cut the melon into bite-sized balls, ensuring they are evenly sized for uniform freezing.
3. Place the melon balls on a tray and place another tray of ice in the freezer to flash freeze them first. This prevents the melon from sticking together in one big chunk when frozen.
4. Once the melon balls have been flash-frozen, transfer them to airtight containers or bags for longer storage.
5. Consider observing how commercial companies handle freezing at the grocery store's frozen foods section to see what techniques they use, as it is likely possible for you to do the same.
Note: Do not attempt to rush the freezing process. Allow adequate time for the melon pieces to freeze thoroughly before storing them in airtight containers or bags.
